title: AI Application Gender Gap Widens: Study Shows Women Use Generative AI 22% Less
excerpt: Recently, actress Reese Witherspoon posted a video on Instagram, urging more women to learn and use artificial intelligence, drawing attention to the gender gap
content_html:
Recently, actress Reese Witherspoon posted a video on Instagram, urging more women to learn and use artificial intelligence, drawing attention to the gender gap in the AI field. She stated that women are underrepresented in the application of artificial intelligence and hopes to promote more women's understanding of this technology. However, the video also sparked some online discussions, with some users arguing that she did not pay enough attention to the environmental impact of AI, data center issues, and algorithmic biases.

Research shows that there is indeed a significant gender gap in AI usage. A meta-analysis published by Harvard Business School in April this year found that based on data from 25 countries and over 143,000 participants, women were 22% less likely to use generative AI than men. A Deloitte study in 2024 also found that as age increases, the trend of AI adoption decline becomes more pronounced, with a particularly significant gender gap among those over 45 years old.

Gender gaps also exist in the workplace. Randstad reports that men make up 71% of AI technology positions, while women account for only 29%. The proportion of men receiving enterprise AI training is 35%, compared to 27% for women; in terms of generative AI skills, 69% of men have these skills, while only 31% of women do.

Experts believe that the low participation of women in AI is related to risk perception, lack of representation in the tech industry, and time resource allocation. Research shows that women often take on more family care responsibilities, leaving them less time to try new technologies, participate in training, and practice continuously.

However, opportunities in the AI era are not limited to core technical positions. Directions such as AI ethics governance, applications in healthcare and education, product operations, and data analysis also provide more entry points for women to participate in the AI industry. Experts suggest that companies should incorporate AI training into their work systems, rather than treating it as an additional burden for employees. Through flexible learning mechanisms, community support, and practical application scenarios, they can help more women develop AI usage capabilities.

As artificial intelligence gradually becomes a fundamental tool for work and life, narrowing the gender gap is not only about technological fairness but may also affect future talent structures and industrial innovation capabilities. Promoting broader AI education and application will become an important issue for the development of the AI ecosystem.

sentences.en: As artificial intelligence gradually becomes a fundamental tool for work and life, narrowing the gender gap is not only about technological fairness but may also affect future talent structures and industrial innovation capabilities.
sentences.zh: 随着人工智能逐渐成为工作和生活的基础工具，缩小性别差距不仅关乎技术公平，还可能影响未来的人才结构和产业创新能力。

sentences.en: Promoting broader AI education and application will become an important issue for the development of the AI ecosystem.
sentences.zh: 推动更广泛的人工智能教育和应用将成为人工智能生态系统发展的重要课题。

content_html_zh:
近日，女演员瑞茜·威瑟斯彭在Instagram上发布了一段视频，呼吁更多女性学习和使用人工智能，引发人们对人工智能领域性别差距的关注。 她表示，女性在人工智能应用方面的代表性不足，希望促进更多女性了解这项技术。 不过，该视频也引发了一些网上讨论，一些用户认为她没有对人工智能对环境的影响、数据中心问题和算法偏差给予足够的重视。

研究表明，人工智能的使用确实存在显着的性别差距。 哈佛商学院今年 4 月发布的一项荟萃​​分析发现，基于 25 个国家和超过 143,000 名参与者的数据，女性使用生成式人工智能的可能性比男性低 22%。 德勤2024年的一项研究还发现，随着年龄的增长，人工智能采用率下降的趋势变得更加明显，其中45岁以上人群的性别差距尤其显着。

工作场所也存在性别差距。 任仕达报告称，男性占人工智能技术职位的 71%，而女性仅占 29%。 接受企业人工智能培训的男性比例为35%，女性为27%；在生成人工智能技能方面，69%的男性拥有这些技能，而只有31%的女性拥有这些技能。

专家认为，女性在人工智能领域的参与度较低与风险认知、科技行业代表性不足以及时间资源分配有关。 研究表明，女性往往承担更多的家庭照顾责任，导致她们尝试新技术、参加培训和持续实践的时间更少。

然而，AI时代的机会不仅仅局限于核心技术岗位。 AI伦理治理、医疗与教育应用、产品运营、数据分析等方向也为女性参与AI行业提供了更多切入点。 专家建议，企业应将人工智能培训纳入工作体系，而不是将其视为员工的额外负担。 通过灵活的学习机制、社区支持、实际应用场景，帮助更多女性培养AI使用能力。

随着人工智能逐渐成为工作和生活的基础工具，缩小性别差距不仅关乎技术公平，还可能影响未来的人才结构和产业创新能力。 推动更广泛的人工智能教育和应用将成为人工智能生态系统发展的重要课题。
